name: james-a-novel
description: Percival Everett's James — a reimagining of Adventures of Huckleberry Finn from Jim's perspective, exploring identity, language as survival, and the act of reclaiming one's story.
useCase: Understanding perspective, navigating identity performance, language and power, confronting injustice, claiming your story.
knownRisks: This is a literary novel — themes are interpretive, apply frameworks with care
Race and systemic injustice are central themes — handle with sensitivity
The novel reimagines a classic — readers may have attachments to the original
